title: DevOps
description: Complete DevOps reference: CI/CD, Kubernetes, Terraform, Ansible, monitoring, cloud, SRE, and more

commands.name: What is DevOps?
commands.code:
DevOps = Development + Operations

Core Principles:
- Collaboration between dev and ops teams
- Automation of everything (build, test, deploy, infra)
- Continuous Integration (CI): merge code changes frequently, auto-test
- Continuous Delivery (CD): auto-deploy to production
- Infrastructure as Code (IaC): manage infra via code
- Monitoring & Observability: know what's happening
- Fast feedback loops: detect issues early

DevOps Lifecycle:
Plan -> Code -> Build -> Test -> Release -> Deploy -> Operate -> Monitor -> (back to Plan)

Key Practices:
- CI/CD Pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI)
- Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible, Pulumi)
- Containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
- Monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK)
- Configuration Management (Ansible, Helm)
- Secrets Management (Vault, SOPS)

commands.name: DevOps Tools Overview
rows: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI, CircleCI, ArgoCD
Git as single source of truth

commands.name: GitHub Actions
rows:
name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test
Run only for PRs to main
- name: Docker build\n  run: docker build -t app:${{ github.sha }} .

docker build -t app:a1b2c3d .
Successfully built abc123
- name: Deploy to EC2\n  run: |\n    ssh deploy@server 'cd /app && git pull && docker-compose up -d'

ssh deploy@server 'cd /app && docker-compose up -d'
Container started successfully

john@pc:~$ gh workflow run deploy.yml --ref main
✓ Workdown "deploy" has been triggered

commands.name: Jenkins Pipeline
rows:
Pipeline created.
Running on Jenkins node.

docker push user/app:latest
The push refers to repository [docker.io/user/app]

commands.name: GitLab CI & CircleCI
rows:
.gitlab-ci.yml
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

build:
  stage: build
  script:
    - docker build -t app .
    - docker push registry.gitlab.com/user/app

Job: build
Docker image pushed to registry
Variables set from GitLab CI/CD settings

.circleci/config.yml
version: 2.1
jobs:
  test:
    docker:
      - image: cimg/node:20.0
    steps:
      - checkout
      - run: npm test
workflows:
  version: 2
  test-deploy:
    jobs:
      - test
      - deploy:
          requires:
            - test

Job 'test' running on cimg/node:20.0
Job 'deploy' waiting for 'test'

commands.name: Pods & Deployments
rows:
# pod.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: nginx:alpine
      ports:
        - containerPort: 80

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f pod.yaml
pod/nginx created

# deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: myapp:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000

john@pc:~$ cat deployment.yaml

commands.name: Services & Networking
rows:
# service.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: web-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: web
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 3000
  type: LoadBalancer

john@pc:~$ kubectl expose deployment web-app --port=80 --target-port=3000
service/web-app exposed
Show all services and their IPs

john@pc:~$ kubectl get svc
NAME          TYPE           CLUSTER-IP      EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)
web-service   LoadBalancer   10.100.20.30    <pending>     80:32000/TCP
kubernetes    ClusterIP      10.100.0.1      <none>        443/TCP

# ingress.yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: app-ingress
spec:
  rules:
    - host: myapp.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: web-service
                port:
                  number: 80

deployment.apps/productpage-v1 created

NAME                              READY   STATUS    RESTARTS
productpage-v1-abc123              2/2     Running   0

# DestinationRule
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: reviews
spec:
  host: reviews
  trafficPolicy:
    connectionPool:
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100

# VirtualService (canary)
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: reviews
spec:
  hosts:
    - reviews
  http:
    - match:
        - headers:
            version:
              exact: v2
      route:
        - destination:
            host: reviews
            subset: v2
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: reviews
            subset: v1
Route 100% of traffic to v1, except header version=v2

commands.name: SLIs, SLOs, SLAs
rows:
SLI = Service Level Indicator
Examples:
- Request latency (p99 < 200ms)
- Error rate (< 0.1%)
- Availability (uptime %)
- Throughput (requests/sec)

SLI: Service Level Indicator
the metric measuring service performance

SLO = Service Level Objective
Example:
- 99.9% of requests complete in < 200ms over 30 days
- 99.99% availability per quarter

SLO: Service Level Objective
your reliability target
SLA = Service Level Agreement\nExample:\n- If uptime < 99.9%, customer gets 10% credit

SLA: Service Level Agreement
external commitment to customers

Error Budget = 100% - SLO
Example:
- SLO: 99.9% uptime
- Error Budget: 0.1% = 43 minutes/month
- If budget exhausted, stop risky deployments

Error Budget Calculation:
Monthly uptime SLO: 99.9%
Allowed downtime: 43m 12s per month

commands.name: Chaos Engineering
rows: # Chaos Monkey (Netflix)\n# Terminates random instances in production\n# Ensures system survives failures

# LitmusChaos in Kubernetes
apiVersion: litmuschaos.io/v1alpha1
kind: ChaosEngine
metadata:
  name: pod-delete
spec:
  appinfo:
    appns: default
    applabel: app=web
  experiments:
    - name: pod-delete
      spec:
        components:
          env:
            - name: TOTAL_CHAOS_DURATION
              value: '30'
            - name: CHAOS_INTERVAL
              value: '10'

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f chaos-engine.yaml
chaosengine.litmuschaos.io/pod-delete created
(expertiment runs, kills random pods)
# Check experiment status\nkubectl get chaosresult -n default

john@pc:~$ kubectl get chaosresult
NAME                              STATUS
pod-delete-pod-delete             Completed
(experiment completed, app survived)

commands.name: Incident Management
rows:
1. Detect (monitoring alert)
2. Respond (acknowledge, page on-call)
3. Diagnose (find root cause)
4. Mitigate (fix/rollback/scale)
5. Resolve (confirm fixed)
6. Learn (postmortem, action items)

# Postmortem template
## Summary
## Timeline
## Root Cause
## Impact
## Action Items
## Lessons Learned

Postmortem documents:
- What happened?
- Why did it happen?
- How do we prevent it?

PagerDuty / OpsGenie / Grafana OnCall
- Define escalation policies
- Set up on-call schedules
- Integrate alert sources
(on-call engineer gets notified of critical alerts)

commands.name: Docker Multi-Stage Builds
rows:
# Multi-stage Dockerfile
FROM node:20 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm ci && npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

john@pc:~$ docker build -t myapp:latest .
Step 1/7 : FROM node:20 AS build
Step 6/7 : COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
Successfully built abc123
john@pc:~$ docker images
myapp        latest    45MB (vs 1.2GB without multi-stage)
# Docker compose build with cache\ndocker build --cache-from=myapp:cache --target=build -t myapp:latest .

john@pc:~$ docker build --cache-from=myapp:cache
=> CACHED [build 1/5] COPY package*.json
=> CACHED [build 2/5] RUN npm ci

# serverless.yml
service: my-api
provider:
  name: aws
  runtime: nodejs20.x
  region: us-east-1
functions:
  hello:
    handler: handler.hello
    events:
      - httpApi:
          path: /hello
          method: get

john@pc:~$ serverless deploy
Deploying my-api to stage dev (us-east-1)
Functions:
  hello: my-api-dev-hello (3.2 MB)
Endpoints:
  GET https://abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/hello
Stack: my-api-dev
Service deployed

john@pc:~$ serverless logs -f hello -t
START RequestId: abc123
2026-05-14T10:00:00Z  INFO  Hello from Lambda!
END RequestId: abc123
# AWS Lambda CLI\naws lambda invoke --function-name my-function --payload '{}' response.json

john@pc:~$ aws lambda invoke --function-name my-function --payload '{}' response.json
StatusCode: 200
ExecutedVersion: $LATEST
# Lambda with API Gateway\n# Lambda URL (direct function URL)\naws lambda create-function-url-config --function-name my-function --auth-type NONE

commands.name: Event-Driven Architecture
rows: # AWS EventBridge rule\naws events put-rule --name my-rule --event-pattern '{"source": ["aws.s3"]}'

# Knative (K8s serverless)
# Service YAML
apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: hello
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - image: gcr.io/knative-samples/helloworld-go

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f service.yaml
service.serving.knative.dev/hello created
john@pc:~$ kubectl get ksvc
NAME     URL                                       READY
hello    http://hello.default.example.com          True

commands.name: Flyway - Database Migrations
rows:
# V1__create_users.sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

# V2__add_orders.sql
CREATE TABLE orders (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
  total DECIMAL(10,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

john@pc:~$ flyway migrate
Flyway Community Edition 10.0.0
Database: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
Successfully applied 2 migrations (execution time 00:00.123s)

john@pc:~$ flyway undo
Flyway Community Edition does not support undo. Use Enterprise edition.

commands.name: Liquibase
rows:
# db/changelog/db.changelog-master.xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<databaseChangeLog xmlns="http://www.liquibase.org/xml/ns/dbchangelog">
  <changeSet id="1" author="john">
    <createTable tableName="users">
      <column name="id" type="int" autoIncrement="true">
        <constraints primaryKey="true"/>
      </column>
      <column name="name" type="varchar(100)"/>
    </createTable>
  </changeSet>
</databaseChangeLog>

john@pc:~$ liquibase update
Liquibase Community 4.27.0
Changeset db.changelog-master.xml::1::john ran successfully

john@pc:~$ liquibase status
3 changesets have not been applied to postgresql@localhost

commands.name: Package Registries (Nexus, Artifactory)
rows: # Sonatype Nexus\n# Supports: npm, maven, pypi, docker, raw\ncurl -u admin:password -X PUT "http://nexus:8081/repository/npm-hosted/package" -H "Content-Type: multipart/form-data" --data-binary @package.tgz
# npm publish to Nexus\nnpm config set registry http://nexus:8081/repository/npm-group/
john@pc:~$ npm config set registry http://nexus:8081/repository/npm-group/
# JFrog Artifactory\n# Multi-format repo manager\ncurl -u admin:password -T myfile.jar "http://artifactory:8082/artifactory/libs-release-local/"

john@pc:~$ cosign sign --key cosign.key 123.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:latest
Pushing signature to: 123.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:sha256-abc123.sig

john@pc:~$ cosign verify --key cosign.pub ...
Verification for 123.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:latest --
The following checks were performed on each of these signatures:
  - The cosign claims were validated

commands.name: OPA (Open Policy Agent)
rows:
# policy.rego
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
  input.request.kind.kind == "Deployment"
  not input.request.object.spec.replicas > 1
  msg := "Deployments must have replicas > 1"
}

john@pc:~$ cat policy.rego
(Policy: Deployments must have >1 replica)

john@pc:~$ curl -X POST http://localhost:8181/v1/data/...
{"result":{"deny":["Deployments must have replicas > 1"]}}

commands.name: Kyverno (K8s Native Policy)
rows:
# require-labels.yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-for-labels
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label app required"
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: "?*"

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f require-labels.yaml
clusterpolicy.kyverno.io/require-labels created

john@pc:~$ kubectl run nginx --image=nginx
Error: admission webhook "validate.kyverno.svc" denied: ...
label app required

commands.name: Falco (Runtime Security)
rows:
# Falco rules
- rule: Terminal shell in container
  desc: Shell spawned in container
  condition: container.id != "" and proc.name = bash
  output: Shell opened in container (user=%user.name)
  priority: WARNING

john@pc:~$ cat falco_rules.yaml
(Rule: detect shell in container)

john@pc:~$ falco
2026-05-14T10:00:00Z: Warning Shell opened in container (user=root)
# Kubernetes audit with Falco\nhelm install falco falcosecurity/falco --set falcosidekick.enabled=true

john@pc:~$ helm install falco ...
(deployed Falco with sidekick for alerts)

commands.name: Feature Flags & Canary Releases
rows: (Feature flag evaluated, path chosen based on flag value)
# Flagger (progressive delivery on K8s)\nkubectl apply -f https://flagger.app/install.yaml

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f ...
namespace/flagger-system created
deployment.apps/flagger created

# Flagger canary config
apiVersion: flagger.app/v1beta1
kind: Canary
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  targetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: myapp
  service:
    port: 80
  analysis:
    interval: 30s
    threshold: 5
    stepWeight: 10
    maxWeight: 100
    metrics:
      - name: request-success-rate
        threshold: 99
    webhooks:
      - name: load-test
        url: http://loadtester.flagger-system/
        timeout: 5s

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f canary.yaml
canary.flagger.app/myapp created
(flagger starts canary rollout)
# Flagger canary status\nkubectl describe canary myapp

Status:
  Canary Weight:  50%
  Failed Checks:  0
  Phase:          Progressing
(gradual 10% increments)
# Flagger promote (manual approval)\nkubectl annotate canary myapp flagger.app/approve=canary

john@pc:~$ kubectl annotate canary myapp flagger.app/approve=canary
canary.flagger.app/myapp annotated
(remaining traffic shifted to new version)

commands.name: Semantic Release & Conventional Commits
rows:
# Conventional commit formats
# fix: -> PATCH version bump
# feat: -> MINOR version bump
# BREAKING CHANGE: -> MAJOR version bump

git commit -m "feat: add login page"
git commit -m "fix: resolve timeout issue"
git commit -m "feat(api): add new endpoint

BREAKING CHANGE: deprecated old endpoint"

john@pc:~$ cat .releaserc.json
(plugins: analyze commits, generate notes, publish)

john@pc:~$ npx semantic-release
[1:25:00 PM] Start step "analyzeCommits"
[1:25:00 PM] Analysis of 2 commits complete: release type: minor
[1:25:00 PM] Published release: v2.1.0 on GitHub
[1:25:00 PM] Published version v2.1.0 to npm

commands.name: Apache Airflow
rows:
john@pc:~$ docker compose up -d
 ✔ Container postgres  Started
 ✔ Container redis     Started
 ✔ Container airflow-init  Started
 ✔ Container webserver  Started
Airflow UI: http://localhost:8080

# DAG definition
from datetime import datetime
from airflow import DAG
from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator

default_args = {
    'owner': 'data-team',
    'retries': 2,
    'retry_delay': timedelta(minutes=5),
}

dag = DAG(
    'data_pipeline',
    default_args=default_args,
    schedule='@daily',
    start_date=datetime(2026, 1, 1),
    catchup=False
)

extract = BashOperator(
    task_id='extract',
    bash_command="python /scripts/extract.py",
    dag=dag
)

transform = BashOperator(
    task_id='transform',
    bash_command="python /scripts/transform.py",
    dag=dag
)

load = BashOperator(
    task_id='load',
    bash_command="python /scripts/load.py",
    dag=dag
)

extract >> transform >> load

john@pc:~$ cat dags/data_pipeline.py
(DAG: extract -> transform -> load)

john@pc:~$ airflow dags list
dags  |  owner  |  runs
------+---------+-------------
data_pipeline  | data-team | 12 success

john@pc:~$ airflow tasks test data_pipeline extract 2026-05-14
...Running <Task: BashOperator: extract>
...Task completed successfully

commands.name: Argo Workflows (K8s Native)
rows: kubectl create namespace argo\nkubectl apply -n argo -f https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/releases/download/v3.5.0/install.yaml

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -n argo -f install.yaml
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/workflows.argoproj.io created

# Argo Workflow YAML
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Workflow
metadata:
  generateName: hello-world-
spec:
  entrypoint: main
  templates:
    - name: main
      dag:
        tasks:
          - name: build
            template: build-app
          - name: test
            depends: build
            template: test-app
          - name: deploy
            depends: test
            template: deploy-app
    - name: build-app
      container:
        image: node:20
        command: [npm, run, build]
    - name: test-app
      container:
        image: node:20
        command: [npm, test]
    - name: deploy-app
      container:
        image: alpine
        command: [echo, "Deployed!"]

john@pc:~$ kubectl apply -f workflow.yaml
workflow.argoproj.io/hello-world-abc123 created

john@pc:~$ argo list
NAME                  STATUS    AGE   DURATION
hello-world-abc123    Succeeded 2m    45s

commands.name: Velero (K8s Backup)
rows: velero install --provider aws --bucket my-backups --backup-location-config region=us-east-1 --snapshot-location-config region=us-east-1 --plugins velero/velero-plugin-for-aws:v1.0.0

john@pc:~$ velero install ...
CustomResourceDefinition/backups.velero.io: created
Deployment/velero: created
Velero is installed! Use kubectl get deployments -n velero to verify.

john@pc:~$ velero backup create daily-backup
Backup request "daily-backup" submitted successfully.
Run `velero backup describe daily-backup` for more details.

john@pc:~$ velero backup get
NAME             STATUS      CREATED                         EXPIRES
weekly-backup    Completed   2026-05-14 10:00:00 +0000 UTC   28d
daily-backup     Completed   2026-05-14 00:00:00 +0000 UTC   30d

john@pc:~$ velero restore create --from-backup daily-backup
Restore request "daily-backup-20260514" submitted successfully.

john@pc:~$ velero schedule create daily --schedule="0 2 * * *"
Schedule "daily" created successfully.
